intent: REWRITE (2026-04-28, after corpus audit; intent text reconciled with shipped artifact 2026-04-28 evening after fresh-eyes subagent audit caught spec/artifact drift). Replaces the previous story 7 ('公園の箱 / The Box in the Park') which had four audited defects: (1) decorative-noun anti-pattern — 鍵 mentioned in s2 then never touched again, (2) empty でも-pivot in s6 (no logical contrast: friend gives book → 'but' I read it), (3) friend with no agency beyond the single transfer, and (4) closer 「私は紙を読みます」 mirrored story 6's closer 「私は本を読みます」 — same `[私]は[N]を読みます` shape. New design retains the box anchor and the nested-container conceit (box → book → paper) — the corpus's first three-level discovery — but rebuilds around real causality. (a) Eliminates the over-anchored 鍵 entirely (it has appeared in stories 1, 3, 5, 6 already; story 7 was its 5th decorative pass). (b) Friend is staged at s0 via companion-と (no teleport) and acts THREE times: takes box, takes book, hands book over. (c) Reflection 「箱は重いです」 matches the era's north-star (「月は白いです。星も白いです。」 — direct attribute description) and adds new tactile information that the visual setting did not establish. (d) でも at s4 introduces real semantic contrast (the box is heavy BUT inside there is just a small old book — the contents undercut the weight expectation), introducing N5_demo (G032_demo) — the rebuilt story's only new grammar point. (e) Closer 「紙は古いです」 is a fresh attribute-attribution beat that retires the warmth-closer template (which had been used in stories 3 and 4); the oldness is narratively resonant — the paper was hidden inside an old book inside a heavy box, so it being old is the discovery. Reinforces all three must-reuse R1 words from story 6 (古い in s4 and the closer, 本 in s4/s5/s6, 読む in s6 narrator's own reading action) AND G012_soshite_then in s5 (友達は本を取ります。そして、私に渡します。 — preserves the brief's example_surface verbatim). Mints exactly 1 new word (重い — fresh i-adjective, fits the tactile/discovery neighborhood). HISTORICAL NOTE: an earlier draft of this story attempted to introduce N5_dictionary_form (G055_plain_nonpast_pair) via the closer 「読む紙は暖かいです」, but the cascade-debt trap documented in AGENTS.md (no story 5–10 organically uses any plain-form verb to reinforce the introduction) forced a redesign — G055 is deferred until at least one story 8+ uses dictionary form. The closer was rotated to 「紙は古いです」 instead, and 重い's reinforcement was absorbed into story 8's reflection 「箱は重いです」 (a one-line corpus-echo edit). The 「私は本を読みます」 sentence at s6 was added to preserve must-reuse W00037 (読む) reinforcement after the closer rotation removed 読む from the final sentence; it does NOT mirror story 6's closer 「私は本を読みます」 because (i) it is not the closer, (ii) it is sandwiched between two friend-driven and two object-discovery sentences, and (iii) the actual closer 「紙は古いです」 is a different shape entirely.

sentences.jp: 朝、私と友達は公園を歩きます。
sentences.en: In the morning, my friend and I walk through the park.

sentences.jp: 木の下に小さい箱があります。
sentences.en: Under the tree there is a small box.
My friend picks up the box.

sentences.jp: でも、箱の中に古い本があります。
sentences.en: But inside the box there is an old book.

sentences.jp: 友達は本を取ります。そして、私に渡します。
sentences.en: My friend takes the book. Then they hand it to me.

sentences.jp: 本の中に小さい紙があります。
sentences.en: Inside the book there is a small paper.
